title: Data Mapping SQL Output Export
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to export my field mappings as SQL SELECT statements, so that I can hand off ready-to-use transformation code to my SQL developer instead of documenting mappings manually.
description:
Generate SQL output from the data mapping work performed in DataTools Pro. Unlike the DDL export (which creates table structures), this feature generates SELECT statements that represent the field-level mapping between source and target systems.

Key behaviors:
- A new "Data Mapping SQL" export option appears alongside existing DDL export
- SQL is generated server-side and formatted client-side using JavaScript
- Mapped fields render as `"SourceObject"."SourceField" AS "TargetField"`
- Picklist-mapped fields render as CASE WHEN statements with individual value mappings
- The source object name is used as the table name in the FROM clause
- A "Copy to clipboard" button is available in the modal
- Fields flagged as "do not migrate" are excluded from the output

This does NOT use GPT — the SQL is deterministically generated from the mapping data.

The SQL syntax targets Snowflake initially, with plans to support other database dialects in the future.
acceptance_criteria: New 'Data Mapping SQL' export option is available
SELECT statement includes all mapped fields with source-to-target aliases
Picklist mappings render as CASE WHEN statements
Source object name is used as the FROM table name
Copy to clipboard button copies the full SQL output
Fields marked 'do not migrate' are excluded from the SQL output
SQL is generated without GPT — deterministic server-side logic
ux_changes: New 'Data Mapping SQL' export option in the export menu. Modal displays formatted SQL with syntax highlighting and a copy-to-clipboard button.
middleware_changes: Server-side SQL generation logic that queries mapping data and constructs SELECT statements. Includes CASE WHEN generation for picklist mappings and field exclusion for 'do not migrate' fields.
api_changes: New endpoint to generate and return the mapping SQL for a given object, based on its source data mappings.
implementation_notes: The SQL generation is entirely server-side (no GPT). JavaScript handles client-side formatting. When no source object is defined, the default table name falls back to 'datatools_pro'. Picklist values appearing in the data table column were disappearing after mapping — this was identified as a separate bug.
comment_summary: Developer had initial FTP connectivity issues for testing. After deployment, refinements were made: source object name used as FROM table name, copy-to-clipboard added, and a bug with picklist values disappearing from the data table was identified as a side effect.
